attributes.description: Alerts when the combined EC2 status check reports a failure (either system or instance sub-check). This is the highest-priority EC2 availability signal.
attributes.name: [AWS EC2 OTel] Status check failed

investigation_guide.blob:
## EC2 Status Check Failed

### Triage
1. Confirm which sub-check failed — run the system vs instance status check alerts for the same instance.
2. **System check failed** → underlying AWS host/hardware issue. Consider **recover** (stop/start on new hardware).
3. **Instance check failed** → guest OS/config issue. Consider **reboot** or investigate OS logs.
4. Check recent deployments, kernel panics, or exhausted memory on the instance.

### Escalation
Status check failures indicate the instance is impaired or unreachable — treat as P1 if production workloads are affected.

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM metrics-awscloudwatchreceiver.otel-*
| WHERE attributes.Namespace == "AWS/EC2"
    AND attributes.MetricName == "StatusCheckFailed"
    AND attributes.stat == "Maximum"
| EVAL ec2_instance = CONCAT(attributes.InstanceId, " (", resource.attributes.cloud.region, ")")
| STATS max_failed = MAX(`metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/EC2/StatusCheckFailed`) BY ec2_instance
// 1 = failed, 0 = healthy — alert on any failure in the window
| WHERE max_failed >= 1
| SORT max_failed DESC
| LIMIT 100
